pattern: Track paint progress with per-row and per-column counters keyed by each value's precomputed coordinates, instead of rescanning the whole grid on every move.
hints: You don't need to repaint the grid and rescan it after every move -- you only need to know how many cells in the specific row and column that were just touched have been painted so far.
Precompute where every value lives in the grid once, then for each painted value bump a per-row and a per-column counter for its coordinates.
The first time a row's counter reaches the row's width, or a column's counter reaches the column's height, that index is your answer.

edge_inputs.description: 1x1 grid, single move completes both the row and the column
single row (m=1): every column has height 1, so the very first move already completes a column
single column (n=1): every row has width 1, so the very first move already completes a row
column-major painting order completes a full column well before any row
row-major painting order completes a full row well before any column

stress.description: random rectangular grid dimensions with a shuffled value layout and a shuffled paint order
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    total = max(2, min(size, 20000))
    m = rng.randint(1, min(200, total))
    n = max(1, total // m)
    total = m * n
    nums = list(range(1, total + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    mat = [nums[i * n:(i + 1) * n] for i in range(m)]
    arr = list(range(1, total + 1))
    rng.shuffle(arr)
    return [arr, mat]
stress.note: arr and mat are independently shuffled permutations of 1..m*n, matching the problem's guarantee
